title: Norway Application for a Residence and Work Permit (Søknad om oppholds- og arbeidstillatelse) — GP-7028 B
description: The Norwegian Directorate of Immigration's (Utlendingsdirektoratet, UDI) form GP-7028 B, "Søknad om tillatelse til arbeid og opphold" ("Application for a permit to work and reside [in Norway]"), the general-purpose application UDI publishes for a first-instance or renewal residence/work permit under any of its four permit grounds: family immigration (section A, including up to a 9-month parental visit), work (section B, including au pair and trainee permits), education (section C), or protection/asylum or another basis (section D). This schema models the form's full seven-page applicant-facing content: personal particulars and contact information (section 1); identity/travel document details and any other persons entered in that document (section 2); prior residence, prior applications, and any prior expulsion from Norway (section 3); the top-level first-time-vs-renewal choice and requested permit duration, followed by all four ground-specific branches — the accompanying family member's own bio data and marriage/cohabitation questions for family immigration, the employer/salary/education-background questions for a work permit, the institution/financing questions for an education permit, and the basis-for-protection questions for section D (section 4); a bounded 6-row foreign-travel history table used only when renewing a permit (section 5); family members even when not themselves applying — a spouse/partner/cohabitant, up to two parents, and up to six other close relatives (children, foster children, or siblings), plus aggregate sibling/child counts (section 6); criminal-record questions (section 7); an optional power of attorney for a representative (section 8); the applicant's own signature block and notification-channel consent (section 9); and, when the applicant is a minor or otherwise not legally competent, up to two parent/guardian signature blocks (section 10). Because the source form's own instruction is simply to "fill in the field(s) that correspond to the basis you are applying on" — with no single dedicated selector widget for which ground(s) apply — this schema introduces four synthetic boolean gating fields (applyingForFamilyImmigration/applyingForWorkPermit/applyingForEducationPermit/applyingForProtectionOrOtherPermit) so each branch's fields can be expressed with this registry's visibleWhen/requiredWhen conventions; see VERIFICATION.md for the full disclosure. This schema excludes the form's own office-use-only content: the top-of-form date/UDI-case-number (DUF-nummer)/fee-paid stamp box (section 1) and the police/foreign-service-station remarks radios (MerknaderGruppe1-3, section 10, headed "Merknader for politiet eller utenriksstasjonen" — Remarks for the police or the foreign service station), since neither is applicant-entered content. It also excludes a disclosed stray duplicate widget layer on pages 4 and 5 (see VERIFICATION.md). Submitting this application is a citizen action performed directly with UDI (in person at a police station/Norwegian foreign mission, or electronically via the udi.no/søknadsportal channel the form's own instructions point to); this schema does not submit the application itself, and the live source (udi.no) is always authoritative. GovSchema is independent and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or operated by the Kingdom of Norway or UDI.

source.documentRef: GP-7028 B (per 1. januar 2010 edition), "Søknad om tillatelse til arbeid og opphold", a 7-page fillable AcroForm PDF published unauthenticated by UDI.

verification.notes: GOV-2340: GovSchema Standard Research cycle. Closes Norway's Visa vertical (6/6) -- the last of Norway's six verticals following GOV-2314/GOV-2316 (Business Formation), GOV-2323 (National ID), and GOV-2330 (DMV); Taxes and Passport were confirmed dead-ends. This form was pre-scouted and named in CATALOG.md's own Known Gaps section (item 8). Fetched fresh (HTTP 200, 1,519,473 bytes, genuine %PDF-1.6 header, no login/CAPTCHA/WAF gate) and independently extracted with pdfjs-dist 3.11.174 (legacy build): 7 pages, 325 raw /Widget annotations, 231 distinct field names (getFieldObjects: 233), matching the pre-extraction recorded in the issue exactly. See this document's own VERIFICATION.md for the full sourcing record, every field-mapping/collapse/exclusion decision (including the four synthetic permit-ground gating fields, the disclosed stray/duplicate widget layers on pages 4-5, and the excluded office-use-only content), and the mock conformance test run.

fields.name: permitDurationRequested

fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section 4 — widget 'Hvor lang tid søker du tillatelse for'

fields.name: applyingForFamilyImmigration
fields.label: Are you applying for a family-immigration permit (section A)?
fields.description: Synthetic gating field introduced by this schema: the source form has no single dedicated selector widget for which of its four permit-ground sections (A family / B work / C education / D protection-or-other) the applicant is completing — its own instruction is simply "fill in the field(s) that correspond to the basis you are applying on," and more than one section can in principle apply at once. This field, and its siblings applyingForWorkPermit/applyingForEducationPermit/applyingForProtectionOrOtherPermit, make that applicability explicit and machine-checkable so the section's fields can be gated with visibleWhen/requiredWhen. See VERIFICATION.md.
fields.sourceRef: Page 2, Section '4 Tillatelsen du søker om' heading 'A Tillatelse til familieinnvandring (inkludert foreldrebesøk i inntil 9 måneder)' (no dedicated widget)

fields.name: spouseSurname

fields.description: Section 6 'Familiemedlemmer' covers family members even if they are not themselves applying for a residence permit; per the form's own instruction this need not be repeated if already given in a previous application, but should be given if other family members are submitting applications at the same time (each family member must still submit their own application form).

fields.name: relative1FullName
fields.label: Relative 1 — surname, first name
fields.description: Section 6 'Andre nære slektninger' (other close relatives): list all children (biological or adopted), foster children, and siblings — use a separate sheet if needed. The source PDF names relative 1's combined surname/first-name widget 'Etternavnet til barn nummer 1' (literally "the surname of child no. 1"), even though — like the other five relatives' widgets, and the shared column header 'Etternavn, Fornavn' ("Surname, First name") spanning the whole row — its rect covers the full combined-name column; this is a disclosed naming quirk in the source PDF, not a second, missing first-name field. See VERIFICATION.md.

fields.label: I grant the following person power of attorney to act on my behalf regarding my application

fields.name: consentToSmsEmailNotifications
fields.label: I agree to receive information about my case by SMS and email

fields.name: applicantSignaturePlaceAndDate
fields.label: Place and date of your signature
fields.description: The source PDF has no separate widget for the wet-ink signature itself, only this place-and-date text field alongside the printed line 'Underskriften din' (Your signature).

fields.name: guardian1SignaturePlaceAndDate
fields.label: Parent/guardian 1 — place and date of signature
fields.description: Section 10 'Når søkeren er barn eller umyndiggjort' (When the applicant is a child or incapacitated) applies only if the applicant is a minor or otherwise not legally competent to sign alone; per the form's own text, if parental responsibility is shared, both parents/guardians must consent. The source form has no dedicated yes/no widget for 'is the applicant a minor,' so these two parent/guardian signature-block fields are left optional rather than gated on a synthetic field.
fields.sourceRef: Page 7, Section 10 — widget 'Sted og dato_2', printed alongside 'Foreldre/verges underskrift' (Parent's/guardian's signature)

fields.name: guardian2SignaturePlaceAndDate
fields.label: Parent/guardian 2 — place and date of signature
fields.sourceRef: Page 7, Section 10 — widget 'Sted og dato_3', printed alongside a second 'Foreldre/verges underskrift' line

documents.statement: Jeg bekrefter at opplysningene som er gitt i denne søknaden er korrekte og fullstendige.
documents.sourceRef: Page 6, Section 9 'Underskrift' signature block (statement has no separate checkbox widget; implicit in signing, same pattern as this registry's other Norway schemas)
